title: Bible or Barber on Abolishing Abortion?
author: Dusty Deevers
orientation: A point-by-point biblical and definitional rebuttal of SBC President Bart Barber and the ERLC's LIGHT Magazine article on ending abortion. Deevers argues that the Pro-Life establishment's refusal to criminalize all parties (including mothers) is a betrayal of Scripture, equal protection, and the SBC's own 2021 Resolution on Abolishing Abortion.
audiences: Skeptics weighing the abolitionist vs. pro-life distinction

questions.question: How does this article define the dividing line between Pro-Life leaders and abortion abolitionists?
questions.audience: Skeptics weighing the abolitionist vs. pro-life distinction
questions.answer: Deevers argues the disagreement is fundamentally definitional: Pro-Life leaders treat abortion as a regulated act performed on a woman who is always a victim, while abolitionists define abortion as prenatal homicide for which any culpable party (mother, doctor, pill industry, accomplices) can be prosecuted under equal protection laws.
questions.quote: ultimately, the division between Barber, the ERLC, and the Pro-Life establishment on one hand and the Abortion Abolitionists on the other comes down to different definitions and foundations of what it means to abolish abortion.

questions.question: What scriptural foundation does the article give for criminalizing abortion as homicide rather than regulating it as healthcare?
questions.answer: Deevers grounds the abolitionist position in the Sixth Commandment, arguing that consistent application of 'You shall not murder' requires extending equal legal protection to preborn lives from fertilization, citing Genesis 9:6, Exodus 21:22-24, and Proverbs 6:17.
questions.quote: Biblically speaking, to abolish abortion is consistently to apply the Sixth Commandment to preborn lives, “You shall not murder” (Exodus 20:13). The Sixth Commandment requires giving equal protection from fertilization/conception by criminalizing the act of abortion as prenatal homicide (Genesis 9:6; Exodus 21:22-24; Proverbs 6:17).

questions.question: What specific position of Bart Barber does the article cite as proof he does not actually want abortion abolished?
questions.answer: The article quotes Barber's own words rejecting prosecution of women who seek or obtain abortions, calling that stance internally contradictory with his claim that 'abortion should be abolished'—comparable to advocating for 'married bachelors.'
questions.quote: Should abortion laws mandate the prosecution of women who seek or obtain an abortion? *I think not. I think it is unjust, unnecessary, and unwise to include in abortion laws the prosecution of women who seek or obtain an abortion*

questions.question: What happened with Louisiana HB 813, and what role did the ERLC play?
questions.answer: HB 813 was a Louisiana abolition bill that would have granted equal protection to all preborn from fertilization. According to Deevers, over 75 Pro-Life organizations—led by Roman Catholics and the ERLC under Brent Leatherwood—released a coalition letter at the exact moment of the floor debate, helping kill the bill.
questions.quote: over 75 Pro-Life organizations, led by Roman Catholics and the ERLC, with Brent Leatherwood at its helm, torpedoed LA House Bill 813, an abolition bill that would have granted equal protection of the laws to all preborn from fertilization in Louisiana.

questions.question: How does the article say ERLC leadership defied the SBC messengers' own 2021 Resolution on Abolishing Abortion?
questions.answer: Deevers contrasts the 2021 Nashville resolution—which rejected exceptions and demanded that 'the murder of preborn children…must be punished equally under the law'—with Leatherwood's anti-equal-protection coalition letter and platform statements that no woman who has an abortion should be 'behind bars.'
questions.quote: The messengers . . . state unequivocally that abortion is murder, and we reject any position that allows for any exceptions to the legal protection of our preborn neighbors, compromises God’s holy standard of justice, or promotes any God-hating partiality. . . . We affirm that the murder of preborn children is a crime against humanity that must be punished equally under the law.

questions.question: Why does the article accuse Barber of giving 'bookless instruction' to a 'people of the Book'?
questions.answer: Deevers charges that Barber's roughly 3,500-word article against abolitionism made almost no use of Scripture, failing the Berean standard of Acts 17:11 and the Titus 1:9 requirement to instruct in sound doctrine.
questions.quote: Barber spends around 3,500 words lobbing numerous charges at the abolitionist movement, scarcely referencing God’s Word.

questions.question: How does the article connect equal protection to the Royal Law of loving one's neighbor?
questions.answer: Deevers argues that to claim love for the preborn while denying them equal legal protection is the partiality James 2:8-9 condemns as sin—meaning fulfillment of the Second Commandment requires criminalizing prenatal homicide for everyone.
questions.quote: If we say we love our preborn neighbor as ourselves, but we show partiality by denying them equal protection of the laws, we deceive ourselves.

questions.question: What slogan does the article propose to truly make abortion 'unthinkable'?
questions.answer: Rather than the ERLC's 'illegal, unnecessary, and unthinkable' framework that still treats abortion as healthcare, Deevers proposes treating it as homicide and criminalizing it for all parties—so that only murderers would do it.
questions.quote: If we really want to make abortion unthinkable, our motto must become, “Make abortion something only murderers would do.” How does a nation do this? By criminalizing the act of prenatal homicide–for everyone.

questions.question: From what moment does the article say human life and equal worth begin, and on what grounds?
questions.answer: Deevers argues life begins at fertilization/conception when God forms the child, and that all humans bear God's image with equal worth that does not vary by ethnicity, age, size, means of conception, development, sex, potential, or social contribution.
questions.quote: all humans display His divine worth, power, and attributes, and thus possess objective and equal worth not varying based on incidental characteristics; such as ethnicity, age, size, means of conception, mental development, physical development, sex (gender), potential, or contribution to society

questions.question: What spiritual posture does the article say abolitionists must take when fighting this battle?
questions.answer: Deevers calls abolitionists to wield the Word of God as a necessity rather than rely on 'puny, pragmatic reasoning,' framing the fight against child sacrifice as spiritual warfare per Ephesians 6 in which success is left to God.
questions.quote: This battle opposing the horrendous cultural evil of child sacrifice is not against flesh and blood, so we must not use puny, pragmatic reasoning. We must fight the Lord’s battles with the spiritual weapons He has put into our hands—and leave the “success” to Him (Ephesians 6:10-20).

related.title: SBC President Bart Barber Gives a Lesson in How Not to Repent
related.reason: Companion Free the States piece by Deevers continuing the critique of Bart Barber's posture toward abolitionists.

related.title: Barber/ERLC Swing and Miss (Again) at Abolitionism
related.reason: Direct follow-up engaging Barber and the ERLC's recurring arguments against abolitionism.

related.title: Former ERLC Policy Director Matt Hawkins Glad SBC Squashed Abolition "Like a Bug"
related.reason: Reinforces the article's claim that ERLC leadership has actively worked to defeat abolition efforts inside the SBC.

related.title: SBC Pastors Publish "Resolution on Abolishing Abortion" for 2021 Convention Meeting
related.reason: Background on the very 2021 Nashville resolution Deevers accuses Leatherwood and Barber of defying.

related.title: Norman Statement
related.reason: The article explicitly directs readers to the Norman Statement as the doctrinal basis for the abolitionist position it defends.

related.title: Equal Protection
related.reason: Expands on the equal-protection-and-criminalization framework that the article uses to define 'abolish.'

related.title: A Mother Is a Magistrate: Why Duress Is No Defense for Abortion
related.reason: Directly answers Barber's 'women are always victims' premise that this article identifies as the heart of Pro-Life resistance to equal protection.
